Name the five primitive data types in OC: How many bits in a nibble and a byte?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: A nibble is 4 bits; a byte is 8 bits

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What range does an 8-bit UNSIGNED integer have?
What range does an 8-bit TWO'S COMPLEMENT integer have?
Why is hexadecimal used as a shorthand for binary?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: One hex digit maps exactly to 4 bits (a nibble), so it is a compact, lossless, error-resistant way to read/write binary; used in memory/MAC addresses and colour codes

Name the five primitive data types in OC: How do you negate a number in two's complement (find -x)?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Invert all the bits (one's complement) then add 1

Name the five primitive data types in OC: In two's complement, what does the MSB represent?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: A NEGATIVE place value (e.g. -128 in 8 bits); if MSB = 1 the number is negative

Name the five primitive data types in OC: How do you perform binary subtraction A - B?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Compute A + (-B) by adding the two's complement of B; discard any final carry out of the MSB

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What is overflow?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: An error where the result of an operation is too large to fit in the available bits (in signed arithmetic, two same-sign numbers giving a wrong-sign result)

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What two parts make up a floating point number?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Mantissa (significand) x 2^exponent

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What does the MANTISSA determine?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Precision / number of significant figures

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What does the EXPONENT determine?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Range / magnitude (where the binary point goes)

Name the five primitive data types in OC: How is a POSITIVE number normalised in floating point?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Mantissa starts 0.1... (a 0 sign bit followed by a 1)

Name the five primitive data types in OC: How is a NEGATIVE number normalised in floating point?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Mantissa starts 1.0... (a 1 sign bit followed by a 0)

Name the five primitive data types in OC: Why do we normalise floating point numbers?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: To remove wasted leading zeros/ones so the mantissa holds the maximum significant figures, giving maximum precision for the available bits

Name the five primitive data types in OC: State the range vs precision trade-off
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: With fixed total bits: more mantissa = more precision but less range; more exponent = more range but less precision. You cannot increase both

Name the five primitive data types in OC: When you move the binary point RIGHT during normalisation, what happens to the exponent?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: It decreases by 1 for each place moved right (the mantissa is multiplied by 2, so the exponent must drop to keep the value the same)

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What is the effect of a LOGICAL SHIFT LEFT by n?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Multiplies by 2^n; zeros fill in from the right; bits shifted off the top are lost (can overflow)

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What is the effect of a LOGICAL SHIFT RIGHT by n?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Divides by 2^n (integer division); zeros fill in from the left; bits shifted off the end are lost

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What is an ARITHMETIC SHIFT RIGHT?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Like a logical right shift but the SIGN BIT is preserved (copied in from the left), so it correctly divides a signed (two's complement) number by 2^n

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What does the bitwise AND operation do and what is it used for?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Output 1 only if both bits are 1; used for MASKING to keep selected bits

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What does the bitwise XOR operation do?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Output 1 if the bits differ; used to TOGGLE bits, simple encryption and checksums

Name the five primitive data types in OC: Describe ASCII
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Originally 7-bit (128 characters); extended ASCII uses 8 bits for 256. Codes are ordered: 'A'=65, 'a'=97, '0'=48

Name the five primitive data types in OC: Describe Unicode and its relation to ASCII
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Uses more bits (e.g. UTF-8, 1-4 bytes) to represent every language plus emoji/symbols; the first 128 codes match ASCII, so it is backward-compatible

Name the five primitive data types in OC: Compare static and dynamic data structures
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Static = fixed size at declaration, contiguous memory (e.g. array). Dynamic = grows/shrinks at run time, allocated from the heap (e.g. linked list)

Name the five primitive data types in OC: Define an array
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: An ordered, fixed-size collection of elements of the SAME data type, accessed by index, stored contiguously giving O(1) random access

Name the five primitive data types in OC: How does a list differ from a tuple?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: A list is mutable and can change size; a tuple is immutable (cannot be changed once created)

Name the five primitive data types in OC: Define a linked list
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: A dynamic structure of nodes, each holding data and a pointer to the next node; a head pointer marks the start and the last node's pointer is null

Name the five primitive data types in OC: Give one advantage and one disadvantage of a linked list
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Advantage: easy insertion/deletion by repointing pointers, grows dynamically. Disadvantage: no random access (must traverse from head); pointer storage overhead

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What does LIFO mean and which structure uses it?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Last In, First Out - a STACK; push/pop at the top

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What does FIFO mean and which structure uses it?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: First In, First Out - a QUEUE; enqueue at the rear, dequeue at the front

Name the five primitive data types in OC: How does a circular queue reuse space?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: The rear (and front) wrap around using modulo arithmetic: rear = (rear + 1) MOD size, reusing slots freed by dequeuing

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What is a priority queue?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: A queue where each item has a priority and the highest-priority item is dequeued first, regardless of arrival order

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What is the ordering property of a binary search tree (BST)?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: For every node, all values in the left subtree are smaller and all in the right subtree are larger than the node

Name the five primitive data types in OC: Name the three BST traversals and what in-order gives
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Pre-order (root, left, right), In-order (left, root, right), Post-order (left, right, root); in-order visits a BST in ascending order

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What is a hash table and how does it achieve fast lookup?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Stores key-value pairs in an array; a hash function converts a key to an array index (e.g. key MOD tableSize), giving near O(1) lookup/insert

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What is a collision and how is it resolved?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Two keys hashing to the same index; resolved by open addressing/linear probing (next free slot) or chaining (a linked list at each slot)

Name the five primitive data types in OC: Compare an adjacency matrix and an adjacency list
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Matrix: 2D array, O(1) edge lookup, best for DENSE graphs but wastes space on sparse. List: each node stores its neighbours, memory-efficient, best for SPARSE graphs but slower edge checks

Name the five primitive data types in OC: State De Morgan's laws
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: NOT(A.B) = NOT A + NOT B, and NOT(A+B) = NOT A . NOT B ('break the bar, change the sign')

Name the five primitive data types in OC: State the Boolean complement (inverse) laws
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: A . NOT A = 0, and A + NOT A = 1

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What are the outputs of a HALF ADDER?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Sum = A XOR B; Carry = A AND B. It cannot accept a carry-in

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What are the outputs of a FULL ADDER?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: Sum = A XOR B XOR Cin; Cout = (A.B) + (Cin.(A XOR B)). It adds three bits and can be chained into a ripple-carry adder

Name the five primitive data types in OC: What is a D-type flip-flop?
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: An edge-triggered 1-bit memory (sequential logic) element that stores the D input on the active clock edge and presents it at Q until the next clock edge; used in registers and counters

Name the five primitive data types in OC: Convert 1101 0110 to hex
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: D6

Name the five primitive data types in OC: Convert denary 172 to 8-bit binary
Convert binary 1010 1100 to hex
Integer, real/float, character (char), s: AC
